description: RIGHT JOIN with multiple left-side columns all becoming nullable

columns.name: user_id
columns.note: LEFT side of RIGHT JOIN: becomes nullable

columns.name: name
columns.note: LEFT side of RIGHT JOIN: becomes nullable

columns.name: email
columns.note: LEFT side of RIGHT JOIN: becomes nullable

name: right_join_left_cols_nullable
query_sql:
-- @name GetOrdersWithUserInfo
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.name, u.email, o.id AS order_id, o.total FROM users u RIGHT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks all left-side columns as nullable due to RIGHT JOIN semantics
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks user_id, name, email as non-nullable based on schema
